CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2015 Continued To Apr-06-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings By Defendant Ph Pham
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE- ATTY. CHIJEH HU OF THE CJH&A ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-510-817-0808 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN D. SHAFFER OF THE MILLER STARR REGALIA, TEL. NO. 1-925-935-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. CHIN T. VO, TEL. NO. 1-510-836-2508 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TP PHAM, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED. "California law, like federal law, favors enforcement of valid arbitration agreements . . . ." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 97.) Bayhills' claims fall within the scope of the arbitration provision, which covers "any dispute or claim . . . arising out of this Agreement or any resulting transaction." (Pham Decl. at Ex. A, 34(B).) The arbitration provision is enforceable under the FAA because Bayhills presents no argument to revoke the contract. (9 U.S.C. 2.) The arbitration provision is also enforceable under the CAA. Because Bayhill requested an entry of default judgment against Nguyen, all of the parties to Bayhill's action are parties to the arbitration agreement and there is no risk of inconsistent rulings if the claims between Bayhills and TP Pham are arbitrated. (Code Civ. Proc., 1281.2, subd.(c).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
